libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Basic Guice injector/module tests can run without special setup. If generated tests exercise method interception, CGLIB Enhancer/MethodProxy, or other proxy-generation paths, configure the test JVM with `--add-opens java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`. Do not add dependencies to recover the old unprefixed internal class names from 2.1.7; in 2.9.1 the main artifact jarjars internal ASM/CGLIB/utility classes with `$`-prefixed names, so tests should prefer public Guice APIs or compile against the actual `$`-prefixed internals if internals are intentionally covered.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 2.1.7 tests import unprefixed internal classes that are not present in the 2.9.1 main jar, so reusing them verbatim will keep failing javac.
AOP/proxy tests may pass with `--add-opens` on the JVM but still need local Gradle and Native Image verification for native-image compatibility.
The `nodeps` classifier contains some unrelocated internals but is not the target artifact and does not provide the bundled CGLIB classes needed for equivalent proxy coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed; sisu-guice brings javax.inject and aopalliance transitively. However, meaningful AOP/proxy-generation coverage on modern JDKs needs the test JVM to open java.lang because the bundled old CGLIB reflectively accesses ClassLoader#defineClass.
